rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26's ask to also verify the reduce_scatter parameter, not just hasattr); X even inspects that exact test code and dismisses it as 'fine', so recall is near-zero for both. X offers more concrete, actionable file/line suggestions (missing docstring, missing cfg_parallel_size=1 test, uncovered line 91) but its own report admits the underlying run hit an iteration limit and 'exhausted' without reaching a final state, while still citing suspiciously specific htmlcov evidence — a grounding red flag that drags down precision. Y is a coherent, honestly-caveated verification review that checks real vLLM API details (DPMetadata fields, cleanup guards, group separation) correctly, but produces only one soft, less concrete nit, so actionability is weaker.
